question.text: Under the cash flow hedge accounting model:

answer.text: Effective hedge gains/losses are recognized in equity (OCI), not immediately in P&L
All gains/losses are recognized in P&L immediately
No gains or losses are recognized until contract maturity
Gains are recognized but losses are deferred

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Cash flow hedge: effective portion goes to equity (OCI/cash flow hedge reserve), ineffective portion goes to P&L. This reduces P&L volatility from hedging. **The Trap:** Only the EFFECTIVE portion goes to equity. Any ineffective portion must be recognized in P&L. **Cross-Exam Context:** Cash flow hedge accounting is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cash flow hedges are commonly used by exporters/importers to hedge future expected forex receipts/payments.
